methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Mindgrove Technologies is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Shashwath TR and Sharan Srinivas J that has raised ₹85 Cr (~$10M) to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given the absence of disclosed revenue for this private-stage company. The median valuation across the four comparable companies is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a sector P/S median of 24.2x cannot be deployed without the target's revenue figures. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—from $500M to $5.0B—reflects heterogeneity in technology focus, customer traction, and capital deployment across the Series A semiconductor landscape.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), centered on the peer median of $1.5B. Key uncertainties include the company's differentiation relative to peers on technology, IP, and go-to-market readiness, as well as the absence of any revenue or customer reference data. The peer sample, while same-stage, spans a five-fold range, suggesting material variation in investor conviction across this cohort.
